The '''''Beta''-class ETR-3 escort transport''' was a medium-sized troop [[Transport/Legends|transport]] vessel produced by [[Telgorn Corporation/Legends|Telgorn Corporation]].

==Characteristics==
Well-armed for its size, its four [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]] and dual [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton-torpedo]] launchers made it a formidable adversary for a lone [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]]. Furthermore, its frontal armament of general-purpose warhead launchers (usually fitted with proton torpedoes), [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] and [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]] made it capable of assaulting or capturing [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] and space stations. Although not as swift as a starfighter, its top speed was comparable with all but the highest-performance cargo and troop transports. Despite being more than twice as large, with all these features—especially the evasive capabilities—ETR-3s were reminiscent of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]–era [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/infantry/Legends|LAAT/i]].

==History==
The ETR-3 was a further development of the [[Gamma-class ATR-6 assault transport|''Gamma''-class ATR-6 assault transport]]. The ETR-3 retained the ATR-6's main hull, giving it approximately the same size and troop-carrying capacity. However, the ETR-3 carried an additional dorsal pod, reintroduced forward-firing laser cannons, and had stronger [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] and armor. Nonetheless, the ETR-3's improvements were not a significant advantage over the ATR-6, which remained the mainstay in the forces of the Imperial Navy. It was also designed to escort construction vessels when establishing planetary outposts on unpacified worlds. With the capacity for carrying up to 40 troops, it was able to transport an entire squad of speeder bikes. The ETR-3's sturdy design, suitable for both military and civilian activities, made it popular throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Capable of significant modification, the craft took on a variety of roles from simple transport to a platform for testing Admiral [[Demetrius Zaarin|Zaarin's]] TIE Experimental Project of advanced and new technology.

Later on, the ETR-3 became a mainstay of [[Death Squadron/Legends|Death Squadron]] and the transport was notable for engaging [[Alliance Starfighter Corps/Legends|Alliance Starfighter Corps]] Squadrons during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].
